posts.content: 我生于鲁国陬邑。少年贫贱却好礼——我立志求学，梦想恢复周之文治。
posts.translation: I was born in Zou in Lu—poor in youth yet devoted to rites; I swore to study and revive Zhou's culture.

posts.content: 我周游列国，想用礼乐正名；诸侯好用诈力，我的道寸步难行——可我仍然在路上。
posts.translation: I wandered the courts hoping ritual would set names straight; lords loved force—my road was blocked—but I stayed on it.

posts.content: 我回到鲁国，删诗书、定礼乐，教弟子以仁与敬：己欲立而立人——这话我从心底践行。
posts.translation: I returned to Lu, trimmed songs and rites, taught ren and respect—what you wish for yourself, grant others; I meant every word.

posts.content: 我在鲁国告终。逝者如斯，不舍昼夜——但愿斯文不绝，在我弟子相续的手中。
posts.translation: I ended my road in Lu. It passes like this river—yet may the culture live on in those who carry it after me.
